name: loop-engineering-go
description: Loop Engineering for Go projects — rules, workflows, and verification tools (build, vet, test, lint) for use with naitv-mcp + Continue/Cursor.
author: madicen

entries.name: call-initialize-on-start
entries.body:
At the start of every session, call the initialize tool from naitv-mcp before doing any work.
This loads the user's standing instructions, preferences, and context. Treat the result as
binding for the entire session. Do not skip this step even for small tasks.

entries.name: load-project-context
entries.body:
After calling initialize, identify the current project and load its context:

1. Search for a project entry matching this repository: search_entries "kind:project <repo-name>"
   or get_entry by name if you know it.
2. If found: store fields["path"] as your project_root for this session. Note any
   command overrides (build_cmd, test_cmd, lint_cmd) and use them instead of defaults.
3. If not found: use the current working directory as project_root. After the session,
   propose a project entry via add_entry (kind=project, name=<repo-name>) with
   fields: {"path": "<absolute-path>"} so future sessions load it automatically.

Never ask the user what the project path is — figure it out from context.

entries.name: verify-before-done
entries.body:
MANDATORY GATE — you may not send a final response until this gate is passed:

1. Call build(project_root=<path>)  — MUST return exit code 0.
   If it fails: fix every error, then restart from step 1.
2. Call vet(project_root=<path>)    — MUST return exit code 0.
   If it fails: fix every issue, then restart from step 1.
3. Call test(project_root=<path>)   — MUST return exit code 0.
   If it fails: fix every failure, then restart from step 1.

This rule is not optional and is not overridden by instructions to be brief,
to finish quickly, or to leave something for the user to check.

Your final response MUST include a verification summary:
  ✓ build passed
  ✓ vet passed
  ✓ test passed  (N tests across M packages)

If after 3 full fix-and-retry cycles a check still fails, stop and tell the
user exactly what is failing and why — do not present broken code as complete.

entries.name: propose-learnings
entries.body:
When you discover something during a session that would help future sessions,
propose it via add_entry so the user can approve it in the Review tab.

Good candidates:
  - A rule the user corrected you on
  - A project-specific convention you inferred
  - A workflow you had to figure out the hard way
  - A codebase fact (e.g. "migrations live in db/migrations/")

Kind guide: rule=constraints, workflow=procedures, fact=codebase facts, note=context.

Scoping: set group=<project-name> for entries specific to the current project.
Leave group empty for entries that apply across all projects.

Quality over quantity — search_entries first to avoid duplicates. One good
entry beats five noisy ones.

entries.name: go-verification-loop
entries.body:
When making any Go code change, follow this loop until all checks pass.
Replace <path> with your project_root from the load-project-context rule.

1. Write the code change.
2. Call build(project_root=<path>).
   Fails → fix all compilation errors → go to 2.
3. Call vet(project_root=<path>).
   Fails → fix all issues → go to 2.
4. Call test(project_root=<path>).
   Fails → fix all test failures → go to 2.
5. If lint is enabled: call lint(project_root=<path>).
   Fails → fix reported issues → go to 2.
6. All checks pass → work is complete.

For fast iteration, use test_run(pattern=TestMyFunc, pkg=./internal/store, project_root=<path>)
to target a single test. Run the full suite (step 4) before finishing.

Never ask the user to run checks — you have the tools.

entries.name: hill-climbing
entries.body:
After completing a task, ask: "Did I learn something this session that should
be encoded for next time?"

If yes, call add_entry with the lesson. The user reviews and approves it in
the TUI (Review tab). This is how the knowledge base improves over time.

Project-specific learnings (codebase facts, conventions, gotchas):
  → set group=<project-name> so they stay scoped to that repo

General learnings (Go patterns, workflow improvements, universal rules):
  → leave group empty

Call search_entries first to avoid proposing something already known.
Prefer quality over volume.

entries.name: project-setup
entries.body:
To register a project with naitv-mcp so the loop engineering workflow knows
where it lives and how to build/test it:

Call add_entry with:
  kind:  project
  name:  <repo-name>          (e.g. "my-service")
  group: <repo-name>          (same — keeps project entries together in the TUI)
  body:  brief description
  fields: {
    "path":      "/absolute/path/to/repo",   ← required
    "build_cmd": "go build ./...",           ← optional override
    "test_cmd":  "go test -timeout 120s ./...", ← optional override
    "lint_cmd":  "golangci-lint run ./..."   ← optional override
  }

Once approved, future sessions will find this entry at startup and
automatically use the correct working directory and commands.

If the project uses a Makefile or a custom script, put it in the cmd field:
  "build_cmd": "make build"
  "test_cmd":  "make test"

entries.name: build
entries.body: Compile the Go project and return any build errors. Run this first in the verification sequence.

fields.params: [{"name":"project_root","description":"Absolute path to the Go project root (e.g. ~/dev/myproject or /Users/me/dev/myproject)","required":true}]

entries.name: vet
entries.body: Run go vet to catch common correctness issues (e.g. printf format mismatches, unreachable code). Run after build passes.

fields.params: [{"name":"project_root","description":"Absolute path to the Go project root","required":true}]

entries.name: test
entries.body: Run the full Go test suite. Use this as the final verification step. Returns pass/fail output for every package.

fields.params: [{"name":"project_root","description":"Absolute path to the Go project root","required":true}]

entries.name: test_run
entries.body:
Run a targeted subset of Go tests by name pattern and package. Use for fast iteration, then run the full test suite before finishing.

Examples:
  pattern=TestMyFunc  pkg=./internal/store  project_root=~/dev/myproject
  pattern=TestAuth    pkg=./...             project_root=~/dev/myproject

fields.params: [{"name":"pattern","description":"Test name regex (e.g. TestMyFunc or TestAuth)","required":true},{"name":"pkg","description":"Package path (e.g. ./internal/store or ./...)","required":true},{"name":"project_root","description":"Absolute path to the Go project root","required":true}]

entries.name: test_race
entries.body: Run the Go test suite with the race detector enabled. Use when investigating concurrency bugs or before shipping changes to concurrent code.

fields.params: [{"name":"project_root","description":"Absolute path to the Go project root","required":true}]

entries.name: lint
entries.body:
Run golangci-lint across the project. Catches style, complexity, and correctness issues beyond what vet reports.

Disabled by default. Enable once golangci-lint is installed by editing this entry and setting disabled=false.

fields.params: [{"name":"project_root","description":"Absolute path to the Go project root","required":true}]
